name: Orellana la Vieja from Playa de las Casetas – circular by the Embalse and Canal de Orellana
name_original: Orellana la Vieja desde Playa de las Casetas – circular por el Embalse y Canal de Orellana
region_intro: Bus station hiking trails around Embalse de Orellana offer diverse landscapes, from the dramatic Sierra de Pela mountain range with its quartzite cliffs and red earth slopes to serene riverbanks and expansive reservoir shores. The region is characterized by a blend of pine and oak woodlands, providing shaded paths, and unique Mediterranean dehesas with scattered oak trees. These varied terrains, including the shores of the Guadiana River, create a rich environment for outdoor exploration. Best bus station hiking trails around Embalse de Orellana The most popular bus station hiking route is Puente Cogolludo from Orellana de la Sierra – round trip through the Parque del Embalse de Orellana and Sierra de Pela, a 12.0 miles (19.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 15 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views of the reservoir and Sierra de Pela. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadiana Natural Trail, a difficult 16.7 miles (26.9 km) path. This route follows the Guadiana River, showcasing the region's landscape transition and passing historical sites. Local hikers also love the Casas de Don Pedro - Canal de las Dehesas Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through dehesas and woodlands, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Embalse de Orellana is defined by its diverse terrain, including the Sierra de Pela mountain range, extensive pine and oak woodlands, and the shores of the Embalse de Orellana. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Embalse de Orellana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Embalse de Orellana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are accessible directly from bus stations around Embalse de Orellana?
A: There are 6 hiking trails in the Embalse de Orellana region that are easily accessible from bus stations. These routes offer a range of distances and difficulties, allowing you to explore the area's diverse landscapes conveniently by public transport.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on bus-accessible hikes in the Embalse de Orellana region?
A: The bus-accessible hikes around Embalse de Orellana feature a variety of terrains. You can expect trails winding through pine and oak woodlands, across unique Mediterranean &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;, and along the serene riverbanks and reservoir shores. Some routes, particularly those near the Sierra de Pela, may also include more rugged paths with hard quartzite cliffs and steep slopes of red earth, offering diverse visual experiences.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options near bus stops for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there is an easy route perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40015092" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana loop from Orellana la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is a gentle 4.9 km walk with minimal elevation gain, starting conveniently from Orellana la Vieja. This allows you to enjoy the natural beauty without a strenuous effort.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes that start and end at a bus station?
A: Absolutely. Several routes are designed as circular paths, allowing you to start and finish your hike at the same point, often near a bus stop. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101829074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Cogolludo from Orellana de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; offers a round trip through the Parque del Embalse de Orellana and Sierra de Pela, providing a comprehensive loop experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I can see along these bus-accessible trails?
A: Many trails offer glimpses of the region's natural and historical highlights. You might encounter the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5667110" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Orellana&lt;/a&gt;, Spain's first inland Blue Flag beach, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5666506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Cogolludo&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also rich in cultural heritage, with sites like the Orellana Palace Castle and the ruins of the Roman city of Lacimurga mentioned in the region's broader hiking context.

Q: Is Embalse de Orellana a good place for birdwatching while hiking from a bus station?
A: Yes, Embalse de Orellana is an excellent destination for birdwatching. The area is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), making it an ornithological paradise. While hiking, especially near cliffs, pastures, and the water body, you have the chance to spot diverse species such as golden eagles, black storks, griffon vultures, and eagle owls.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike from bus stations in Embalse de Orellana?
A: The best times to hike in Embalse de Orellana are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder, making for more pleasant hiking conditions. The shaded trails through pine and oak woodlands are particularly enjoyable, and the natural landscapes are vibrant.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes accessible by bus for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the region offers several difficult routes accessible by bus. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1266935539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a substantial 26.9 km route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1101829074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Cogolludo from Orellana de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; covers over 19 km with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the bus-accessible trails in Embalse de Orellana?
A: The trails around Embalse de Orellana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.04 stars. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil reservoir views to the rugged Sierra de Pela, and the convenience of accessing these natural wonders via public transport.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails accessible from bus stops?
A: Yes, for those looking for a balanced challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1102629445" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casas de Don Pedro - Canal de las Dehesas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.8 km route. It offers a good distance and manageable elevation, perfect for a satisfying day hike without being overly strenuous.
tips: The Orellana reservoir is a reservoir on the Guadiana river in the province of Badajoz. It is the largest of those located in the middle section of the river, it is regulated by those of García de Sola and Cíjara and by the transfer of Zújar and La Serena.

In the municipality of Orellana la Vieja is the first inland beach in all of Spain with a Blue Flag for its accessibility, the quality of its waters and the environmental information offered to users. In addition, it is also a blue trail and marina.

It is a 15th century military fortress rebuilt as a palace in the 16th century and built in the municipality of Orellana la Vieja.
The building's patio stood out, formed by two galleries supported by columns, semicircular arches and rectilinear lintels on the main floor.

Today two towers remain, one circular and the other square and taller, the keep. Also, you can see the shield of the Altamirano, arches, supports with Ionic capitals and remains of towers. Although part of the castle was destroyed to convert it into a private home after the civil war.

It has been declared a historical and cultural heritage of Extremadura and in the town it is known simply as "the palace".

waypoints.name: Castle and Palace of the Orellana
